summary: Demand evidence agents completed and PRD readiness passed.

demand_status.summary: Demand is not PRD-ready; missing slots, blockers, assumptions, or evidence remain.

triage.explanation: Fast route by default; no hard factual risk trigger was found.

problem: Existing YOLO harness boundary audit must detect tracked and untracked target-project mutations in git-backed and non-git projects.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Boundary snapshots cover git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks and exclude only the dispatch artifact root.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Boundary snapshots cover git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks and exclude only the dispatch artifact root.
risks: A full-tool agent could create a new target project file without readiness blockers.

blockers.message: explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch boundary snapshot implementation covers git ls-files tracked/cached/other files, includes fallback walks for non-git projects, and excludes only the dispatch artifact root.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot combines git ls-files with recursive filesystem walk, excluding only allowed write roots
evidence.why: Core implementation of boundary snapshot logic; combines git and fallback strategies

evidence.summary: gitFiles runs git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z to capture tracked, cached, and other non-excluded files
evidence.why: Confirms git ls-files covers tracked (-c), other (-o), and respects .gitignore (--exclude-standard)

evidence.summary: walkFiles recursively walks directory tree as fallback for non-git projects or untracked files
evidence.why: Provides fallback coverage for non-git projects and gitignored files outside excluded dirs

evidence.summary: excludedDir skips .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache directories
evidence.why: Documents excluded directories; fallback walk respects these exclusions

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot merges git results with walkFiles, then filters out allowedRoots via isWithin check
evidence.why: Combines git and fallback strategies while excluding allowed artifact roots

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot called with allowedRoots=[plan.output_dir] to exclude dispatch artifact root
evidence.why: Runtime call site confirming dispatch artifact root is the only allowed write root

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after snapshots and detects added, modified, deleted files
evidence.why: Confirms mutation detection logic identifies all change types

evidence.summary: boundaryAfter computed and diffed against boundaryBefore; violations trigger BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers
evidence.why: Runtime enforcement that boundary violations block readiness

evidence.summary: git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations
evidence.why: Test confirms untracked file mutations are detected in git repos

evidence.summary: git-backed boundary detects gitignored project mutations outside excluded dirs
evidence.why: Test confirms gitignored file mutations (like .env) are detected if outside excluded dirs

evidence.summary: non-git boundary fallback detects modified project files and ignores artifacts
evidence.why: Test confirms non-git projects use walkFiles fallback and exclude allowed artifact roots

evidence.summary: boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass
evidence.why: Test confirms boundary violations override successful evidence agent results

evidence.summary: BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers added to readiness and prd_ready set to false
evidence.why: Confirms boundary violations become readiness blockers

evidence.summary: single_agent evidence policy requires explorer and verifier agents
evidence.why: Confirms evidence agent roles for brownfield/hybrid demands

risks: A full-tool agent with file write permissions could create a new target project file before the boundary snapshot runs, bypassing detection if the snapshot only compares against tracked files.

result.notes: Boundary snapshot implementation verified: combines git ls-files (tracked/cached/other) with recursive walkFiles fallback, filters out allowed artifact roots only, detects all mutation types. Test coverage confirms git-backed and non-git scenarios. Risk of pre-snapshot mutation by full-tool agents remains a timing concern, not a coverage gap.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch boundary snapshot implementation covers git ls-files tracked/cached/other files, includes fallback walks for non-git projects, and excludes only the dispatch artifact root.

evidence.summary: gitFiles uses git ls-files -co --exclude-standard to capture tracked, cached, and other files
evidence.why: Primary evidence that git ls-files flags cover all required file types

evidence.summary: walkFiles provides recursive filesystem walk as fallback for non-git projects
evidence.why: Verifies fallback coverage for non-git scenarios

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot merges git and walk results, filters via isWithin against allowedRoots
evidence.why: Confirms combination strategy and allowed root exclusion

evidence.summary: Runtime call passes allowedRoots=[plan.output_dir] as only exclusion
evidence.why: Runtime evidence that only dispatch artifact root is excluded

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after to detect added, modified, deleted files
evidence.why: Verifies mutation detection covers all change types

evidence.summary: Test case verifies git-backed boundary detects untracked mutations
evidence.why: Test evidence for untracked file detection

evidence.summary: Test case verifies git-backed boundary detects gitignored mutations outside excluded dirs
evidence.why: Test evidence for gitignored file detection

evidence.summary: Test case verifies non-git fallback detects mutations while ignoring artifacts
evidence.why: Test evidence for non-git fallback coverage

risks: Full-tool agent could create target project file after beforeSnapshot but before agent execution, making it appear in both snapshots and avoid detection as a mutation

result.notes: Explorer evidence verified: all 13 records cite actual project files with correct line ranges, scope classifications are accurate (all project-scoped), no assumptions promoted as facts. Test coverage confirms git-backed tracked/untracked/gitignored detection and non-git fallback. The pre-snapshot timing risk identified by explorer is accurately categorized as risk, not blocker.
